lines.gurmukhi: ਪਹਿਰੇ ਪਟੰਬਰ ਕਰਿ ਅਡੰਬਰ ਆਪਣਾ ਪਿੜੁ ਮਲੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: pahire pattanbar kar addanbar aapanaa pirr maleeai |
lines.english: Let us wear our virtues like silk clothes; let us decorate ourselves, and enter the arena.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਝ ਕਰੀਜੈ ਗੁਣਹ ਕੇਰੀ ਛੋਡਿ ਅਵਗਣ ਚਲੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: saajh kareejai gunah keree chhodd avagan chaleeai |
lines.english: Let us form a partnership, and share our virtues; let us abandon our faults, and walk on the Path.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਥੈ ਜਾਇ ਬਹੀਐ ਭਲਾ ਕਹੀਐ ਝੋਲਿ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਪੀਜੈ ॥
lines.translit: jithai jaae baheeai bhalaa kaheeai jhol amrit peejai |
lines.english: Let us speak of goodness, wherever we go and sit; let us skim off the Ambrosial Nectar, and drink it in.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਣਾ ਕਾ ਹੋਵੈ ਵਾਸੁਲਾ ਕਢਿ ਵਾਸੁ ਲਈਜੈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: gunaa kaa hovai vaasulaa kadt vaas leejai |3|
lines.english: One who has a basket of fragrant virtues, should enjoy its fragrance. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਿ ਕਰੇ ਕਿਸੁ ਆਖੀਐ ਹੋਰੁ ਕਰੇ ਨ ਕੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: aap kare kis aakheeai hor kare na koee |
lines.english: He Himself acts; unto whom should we complain? No one else does anything.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਖਣ ਤਾ ਕਉ ਜਾਈਐ ਜੇ ਭੂਲੜਾ ਹੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: aakhan taa kau jaaeeai je bhoolarraa hoee |
lines.english: Go ahead and complain to Him, if He makes a mistake.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਹੋਇ ਭੂਲਾ ਜਾਇ ਕਹੀਐ ਆਪਿ ਕਰਤਾ ਕਿਉ ਭੁਲੈ ॥
lines.translit: je hoe bhoolaa jaae kaheeai aap karataa kiau bhulai |
lines.english: If He makes a mistake, go ahead and complain to Him; but how can the Creator Himself make a mistake?

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਣੇ ਦੇਖੇ ਬਾਝੁ ਕਹਿਐ ਦਾਨੁ ਅਣਮੰਗਿਆ ਦਿਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: sune dekhe baajh kahiai daan anamangiaa divai |
lines.english: He sees, He hears, and without our asking, without our begging, He gives His gifts.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਿ ਕਰੇ ਕਿਸੁ ਆਖੀਐ ਹੋਰੁ ਕਰੇ ਨ ਕੋਈ ॥੪॥੧॥੪॥
lines.translit: aap kare kis aakheeai hor kare na koee |4|1|4|
lines.english: He Himself acts; unto whom should we complain? No one else does anything. ||4||1||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਾਨੁ ਦੇਇ ਦਾਤਾ ਜਗਿ ਬਿਧਾਤਾ ਨਾਨਕਾ ਸਚੁ ਸੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: daan dee daataa jag bidhaataa naanakaa sach soee |
lines.english: The Great Giver, the Architect of the Universe, gives His gifts. O Nanak, He is the True Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਪਉੜੀ ਸਾਚ ਕੀ ਸਾਚਾ ਸੁਖੁ ਹੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: gur kee paurree saach kee saachaa sukh hoee |
lines.english: Truth is the ladder to the Guru; climbing up to the True Lord, peace is obtained.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੂਹੀ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: soohee mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Soohee, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੇਰਾ ਮਨੁ ਰਾਤਾ ਗੁਣ ਰਵੈ ਮਨਿ ਭਾਵੈ ਸੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: meraa man raataa gun ravai man bhaavai soee |
lines.english: My mind is imbued with His Glorious Praises; I chant them, and He is pleasing to my mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਸਨਾਨੁ ਦਾਨੁ ਸੁਗਿਆਨੁ ਮਜਨੁ ਆਪਿ ਅਛਲਿਓ ਕਿਉ ਛਲੈ ॥
lines.translit: eisanaan daan sugiaan majan aap achhalio kiau chhalai |
lines.english: He Himself is Undeceivable; how could He ever be deceived by cleansing baths, charity, spiritual wisdom or ritual bathings?

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਖਿ ਸਹਜਿ ਆਵੈ ਸਾਚ ਭਾਵੈ ਸਾਚ ਕੀ ਮਤਿ ਕਿਉ ਟਲੈ ॥
lines.translit: sukh sehaj aavai saach bhaavai saach kee mat kiau ttalai |
lines.english: Celestial peace comes; the Truth pleases me. How could these True Teachings ever be erased?

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਰਪੰਚ ਮੋਹ ਬਿਕਾਰ ਥਾਕੇ ਕੂੜੁ ਕਪਟੁ ਨ ਦੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: parapanch moh bikaar thaake koorr kapatt na doee |
lines.english: Fraud, attachment and corruption are taken away, as are falsehood, hypocrisy and duality.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੇਰਾ ਮਨੁ ਰਾਤਾ ਗੁਣ ਰਵੈ ਮਨਿ ਭਾਵੈ ਸੋਈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: meraa man raataa gun ravai man bhaavai soee |1|
lines.english: My mind is imbued with His Glorious Praises; I chant them, and He is pleasing to my mind. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਹਿਬੁ ਸੋ ਸਾਲਾਹੀਐ ਜਿਨਿ ਕਾਰਣੁ ਕੀਆ ॥
lines.translit: saahib so saalaaheeai jin kaaran keea |
lines.english: So praise your Lord and Master, who created the creation.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੈਲੁ ਲਾਗੀ ਮਨਿ ਮੈਲਿਐ ਕਿਨੈ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਪੀਆ ॥
lines.translit: mail laagee man mailiai kinai amrit peea |
lines.english: Filth sticks to the polluted mind; how rare are those who drink in the Ambrosial Nectar.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਥਿ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਪੀਆ ਇਹੁ ਮਨੁ ਦੀਆ ਗੁਰ ਪਹਿ ਮੋਲੁ ਕਰਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: math amrit peea ihu man deea gur peh mol karaaeaa |
lines.english: Churn this Ambrosial Nectar, and drink it in; dedicate this mind to the Guru, and He will value it highly.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਨੜਾ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਸਹਜਿ ਪਛਾਤਾ ਜਾ ਮਨੁ ਸਾਚੈ ਲਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: aapanarraa prabh sehaj pachhaataa jaa man saachai laaeaa |
lines.english: I intuitively realized my God, when I linked my mind to the True Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸੁ ਨਾਲਿ ਗੁਣ ਗਾਵਾ ਜੇ ਤਿਸੁ ਭਾਵਾ ਕਿਉ ਮਿਲੈ ਹੋਇ ਪਰਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: tis naal gun gaavaa je tis bhaavaa kiau milai hoe paraaeaa |
lines.english: I will sing the Lord's Glorious Praises with Him, if it pleases Him; how could I meet Him by being a stranger to Him?

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਹਿਬੁ ਸੋ ਸਾਲਾਹੀਐ ਜਿਨਿ ਜਗਤੁ ਉਪਾਇਆ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: saahib so saalaaheeai jin jagat upaaeaa |2|
lines.english: So praise your Lord and Master, who created the creation. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਇ ਗਇਆ ਕੀ ਨ ਆਇਓ ਕਿਉ ਆਵੈ ਜਾਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: aae geaa kee na aaeo kiau aavai jaataa |
lines.english: When He comes, what else remains behind? How can there be any coming or going then?

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰੀਤਮ ਸਿਉ ਮਨੁ ਮਾਨਿਆ ਹਰਿ ਸੇਤੀ ਰਾਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: preetam siau man maaniaa har setee raataa |
lines.english: When the mind is reconciled with its Beloved Lord, it is blended with Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਹਿਬ ਰੰਗਿ ਰਾਤਾ ਸਚ ਕੀ ਬਾਤਾ ਜਿਨਿ ਬਿੰਬ ਕਾ ਕੋਟੁ ਉਸਾਰਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: saahib rang raataa sach kee baataa jin binb kaa kott usaariaa |
lines.english: True is the speech of one who is imbued with the Love of his Lord and Master, who fashioned the body fortress from a mere bubble.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੰਚ ਭੂ ਨਾਇਕੋ ਆਪਿ ਸਿਰੰਦਾ ਜਿਨਿ ਸਚ ਕਾ ਪਿੰਡੁ ਸਵਾਰਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: panch bhoo naaeiko aap sirandaa jin sach kaa pindd savaariaa |
lines.english: He is the Master of the five elements; He Himself is the Creator Lord. He embellished the body with Truth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਮ ਅਵਗਣਿਆਰੇ ਤੂ ਸੁਣਿ ਪਿਆਰੇ ਤੁਧੁ ਭਾਵੈ ਸਚੁ ਸੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: ham avaganiaare too sun piaare tudh bhaavai sach soee |
lines.english: I am worthless; please hear me, O my Beloved! Whatever pleases You is True.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਵਣ ਜਾਣਾ ਨਾ ਥੀਐ ਸਾਚੀ ਮਤਿ ਹੋਈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: aavan jaanaa naa theeai saachee mat hoee |3|
lines.english: One who is blessed with true understanding, does not come and go. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਜਨੁ ਤੈਸਾ ਅੰਜੀਐ ਜੈਸਾ ਪਿਰ ਭਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: anjan taisaa anjeeai jaisaa pir bhaavai |
lines.english: Apply such an ointment to your eyes, which is pleasing to your Beloved.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਿ ਜਾਣਾਵੈ ਮਾਰਗਿ ਪਾਵੈ ਆਪੇ ਮਨੂਆ ਲੇਵਏ ॥
lines.translit: aap jaanaavai maarag paavai aape manooaa leve |
lines.english: He Himself shows me the Way, and He Himself leads me to it, attracting my mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਮਝੈ ਸੂਝੈ ਜਾਣੀਐ ਜੇ ਆਪਿ ਜਾਣਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: samajhai soojhai jaaneeai je aap jaanaavai |
lines.english: I realize, understand and know Him, only if He Himself causes me to know Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੰਤੁ ਮੰਤੁ ਪਾਖੰਡੁ ਨ ਜਾਣਾ ਰਾਮੁ ਰਿਦੈ ਮਨੁ ਮਾਨਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: tant mant paakhandd na jaanaa raam ridai man maaniaa |
lines.english: I know nothing of Tantric spells, magical mantras and hypocritical rituals; enshrining the Lord within my heart, my mind is satisfied.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਮ ਸੁਕਰਮ ਕਰਾਏ ਆਪੇ ਕੀਮਤਿ ਕਉਣ ਅਭੇਵਏ ॥
lines.translit: karam sukaram karaae aape keemat kaun abheve |
lines.english: He Himself causes us to do good and bad deeds; who can know the value of the Mysterious Lord?

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਜਨ ਰਾਤੇ ਸਚ ਕੇ ਸੰਗੇ ਮਨ ਮਾਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: saajan raate sach ke sange man maahee |
lines.english: My friends are imbued with the True Lord; He is with them, in their minds.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਜਨੁ ਨਾਮੁ ਤਿਸੈ ਤੇ ਸੂਝੈ ਗੁਰਸਬਦੀ ਸਚੁ ਜਾਨਿਆ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: anjan naam tisai te soojhai gurasabadee sach jaaniaa |4|
lines.english: The ointment of the Naam, the Name of the Lord, is only understood by one who realizes the Lord, through the Word of the Guru's Shabad. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਜਨ ਹੋਵਨਿ ਆਪਣੇ ਕਿਉ ਪਰ ਘਰ ਜਾਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: saajan hovan aapane kiau par ghar jaahee |
lines.english: I have my own friends; why should I go to the home of a stranger?

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨ ਮਾਹਿ ਸਾਜਨ ਕਰਹਿ ਰਲੀਆ ਕਰਮ ਧਰਮ ਸਬਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: man maeh saajan kareh raleea karam dharam sabaaeaa |
lines.english: In their minds, these friends celebrate in happiness; all good karma, righteousness and Dharma,
